sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 50-year-old male, presents with a 2-year history of back pain that started after moving furniture.
He describes the pain as a sharp stabbing pain that radiates down to his right leg, including his big toe.
He also reports numbness and tingling in his right leg, especially in the foot, and difficulty getting comfortable when sitting.
He denies any numbness or tingling in the genital or rectal area, weakness, loss of bladder or bowel control, or family history of spine conditions.
He has tried physical therapy and strong pain medications in the past, but has avoided surgery.
He has gained weight due to limited activity and is concerned about returning to his hobby of golf.
Past medical history: The patient is a former smoker, having quit 20 years ago.
He has not mentioned any other medical conditions or relevant social history.
Review of systems: The patient reports no other symptoms in other organs that are relevant to his initial presentation.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has no overlying erythema or ecchymosis on the musculoskeletal back exam.
There is midline tenderness at L4-L5 disk space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
The patient experiences pain with lumbar flexion and a positive straight leg test.
The neurological examination reveals decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at L4 dermatome.
Patellar and Achilles reflexes are symmetrical.
Test Results: The MRI results show disk herniation with nerve impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has an L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy, causing symptoms consistent with nerve impingement.
Problem list: Herniated disk L4-L5; Radiculopathy; Chronic lumbar back pain; Weight gain; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed a spinal injection with a corticosteroid to help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
The corticosteroid will be injected into the lumbar spine under fluoroscopy.
The patient will be monitored for response to the injection, with subsequent injections limited to no more than four times a year if necessary.
Follow-up: The patient will be scheduled for a follow-up appointment to monitor response to the injection and determine if additional injections are needed.
